meta.feed_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

items.title: Macron sings as Armenia’s leader drums in unusual moment at state dinner
items.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron sang ‘La Boheme’ accompanied by Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan on the drums.

items.title: Russian air attacks kill five at Ukraine’s Naftogaz gas facilities
items.summary: Three workers and two emergency service rescuers ​killed and 37 others wounded, Naftogaz CEO Serhiy ​Koretskyi says.

items.title: Taiwan has ‘right to engage with the world’ after Eswatini visit
items.summary: Taiwan has ‘right to engage with the world’ after Eswatini visit

items.title: NBA semifinal series: Timberwolves stun Spurs in Game 1; Knicks beat 76ers
items.summary: Anthony Edwards returned from injury to score 18 points as the visitors beat San Antonio Spurs 104-102.

items.title: What Iran is saying about attack on UAE
items.summary: What Iran is saying about attack on UAE

items.title: Afghanistan accuses Pakistan of killing three civilians in ‘war crime’
items.summary: The incident is the latest test of a fragile ceasefire agreed between the two countries last month.

items.title: What to know ahead of the Ohio primary election
items.summary: Several races being closely watched as both parties prepare for the crucial midterm elections in November.

items.title: As summer approaches, skin diseases spread through Gaza’s refugee camps
items.summary: Skin conditions have become a serious issue for healthcare workers in Gaza, particularly when temperatures rise.

items.title: In Mauritania, push to phase out private schools divides opinion
items.summary: Officials say measure will ease systemic discrimination, but private school sector fears education quality is at risk.

items.title: Palestine weekly wrap: Israeli security agencies sound alarm on settlers
items.summary: Israeli officials warn that violence threatens state, but attacks, land seizures and blockades continue in West Bank.

items.title: No FIFA World Cup 2026 broadcast deals signed in India, China
items.summary: Discussions on the sale of media rights in both countries are ongoing, FIFA says weeks before the World Cup begins.

items.title: Nine workers killed in Colombia coal mine explosion
items.summary: The national mining ​agency had warned weeks earlier that a buildup of gases at the mine could become dangerous.

items.title: Thiago Ávila’s letter from Israeli prison to his daughter
items.summary: The Brazilian activist explains to his little girl his motivation to join the Gaza-bound humanitarian flotilla.

items.title: Explosion at fireworks factory in China kills 26, injures 61
items.summary: Rescue efforts are still under way after a huge explosion ripped through China's fireworks capital.

items.title: ‘What comes next?’: Fear grips Pakistani families of Somali piracy victims
items.summary: As piracy resurges off Somalia, families of hijacked tanker crew in Pakistan search for answers and help.

items.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 67 as Hormuz crisis deepens?
items.summary: The UAE says it intercepted Iranian missiles after a suspected drone attack, with no comment from Iran.

items.title: FIFA bans former Guyana official Alves for 5 years over sexual harassment
items.summary: Former General Secretary Ian Alves found guilty of sexually harassing female staff ​members by FIFA's Ethics Committee.

items.title: Iran says US military killed five civilians in attacks on passenger boats
items.summary: Iranian commander says US military attacked two passenger boats, not IRGC vessels, in the Strait of Hormuz on Monday.

items.title: Man City have lost control of the Premier League title race: Guardiola
items.summary: Man City's 3-3 draw at Everton has left them five points adrift of leaders Arsenal, albeit with a game in hand.

items.title: A small plane crashes into building in Brazil, killing three
items.summary: A small plane carrying five people crashed into a residential building in Belo Horizonte, Brazil, shortly after takeoff.

items.title: States should tax windfall oil profits to fund their way out of crisis
items.summary: A major economic crisis is boosting energy profits. Taxing those can help countries survive and become immune to shocks.

items.title: An explosion at a fireworks plant in China has killed at least 21 people
items.summary: An explosion at a fireworks plant in a central Chinese province has killed at least 21 people and injured 61 others.

items.title: ‘Dangerous escalation’: World condemns Iran after attacks on UAE
items.summary: Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Kuwait and Bahrain denounce strikes, as Germany, UK and Canada urge Iran to return to talks.

items.title: Oil prices surge as violence flares in Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Brent crude rises nearly 6 percent as soaring tensions in waterway push ceasefire to the brink.

items.title: Strong security presence in Mexico’s Sinaloa state amid cartel violence
items.summary: Security forces have intensified their presence across parts of Mexico’s Sinaloa.
